sourceTitle: Get answers about work content faster with Q&A

questions.question: How do you open the Q&A assistant in Notion?

options.text: Click the sparkle icon in the bottom‑right corner of the Notion window
options.explanation: The guide explicitly says to click the sparkle in the bottom right to open Q&A.

options.text: Press Ctrl + F on your keyboard
options.explanation: Ctrl + F opens the browser’s find‑in‑page search, not the Q&A assistant.

options.text: Open the Settings menu and select “Q&A”
options.explanation: There is no Settings entry for Q&A; the assistant is launched via the sparkle icon or the search menu.

options.text: Navigate to the Help Center and click “Ask Q&A”
options.explanation: The Help Center is a separate resource; Q&A is accessed directly from within the workspace.

questions.question: Which Notion AI tool is primarily used for drafting, editing, brainstorming, and research?

options.text: Writer
options.explanation: Writer is described in the guide as the tool for drafting, editing, brainstorming and research.

options.text: Q&A
options.explanation: Q&A is for retrieving information from your workspace, not for creating new content.

options.text: Autofill
options.explanation: Autofill works at the database level to extract and summarize key information.

options.text: Notion Docs
options.explanation: Notion Docs is not an AI tool; it’s a general term for pages in Notion.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of Q&A for engineers according to the guide?

options.text: It lets them quickly unblock themselves by retrieving instant answers from internal docs
options.explanation: Engineers can avoid context‑switching and get immediate answers, keeping their flow intact.

options.text: It automatically writes production‑ready code for them
options.explanation: Q&A provides information, not code generation.

options.text: It designs UI mock‑ups based on requirements
options.explanation: Design mock‑ups are a designer’s use‑case, not an engineer’s primary benefit.

options.text: It schedules meetings with product managers
options.explanation: Scheduling meetings is unrelated to the Q&A feature described.

questions.question: How does Q&A respect security and privacy within a Notion workspace?

options.text: It only returns information from pages the user already has permission to view
options.explanation: The guide notes that Q&A respects your security by limiting results to content you can access.

options.text: It shares all workspace data with Notion’s cloud servers for processing
options.explanation: While processing occurs in the cloud, the assistant does not expose data beyond your access rights.

options.text: It encrypts every query but still shows results from any page in the workspace
options.explanation: Encryption alone would not prevent unauthorized data exposure; Q&A limits results by permissions.

options.text: It automatically grants the user read‑only access to every page in the workspace
options.explanation: Q&A does not change permissions; it works within existing access controls.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a suggested use case for Q&A in the guide?

options.text: Summarizing a meeting you missed
options.explanation: The guide explicitly mentions using Q&A to get a summary of missed meetings.

options.text: Pulling up campaign data on the fly
options.explanation: Marketing teams are encouraged to ask Q&A for campaign metrics.

options.text: Generating new design assets such as logos
options.explanation: Creating visual assets is not mentioned; Q&A focuses on retrieving textual information.

options.text: Answering onboarding questions for new hires
options.explanation: Onboarding assistance is a highlighted scenario for Q&A.

questions.question: To get the most value from Q&A, a company should ensure its Notion workspace is:

options.text: Comprehensive, up‑to‑date, and connected across teams
options.explanation: The guide stresses that a well‑populated, current, and linked workspace makes Q&A more powerful.

options.text: Limited to only marketing content
options.explanation: Restricting content would reduce Q&A’s usefulness for other roles.

options.text: Isolated per team with no shared pages
options.explanation: Isolation prevents Q&A from surfacing cross‑team knowledge.

options.text: Encrypted so that AI cannot read any text
options.explanation: If the AI cannot read the content, it cannot provide answers.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between Writer and Q&A as described in the guide?

options.text: Writer pulls in external knowledge while Q&A only uses information stored in your workspace
options.explanation: Writer can look outside Notion for knowledge; Q&A is limited to your internal docs.

options.text: Writer can edit database rows, whereas Q&A cannot interact with databases at all
options.explanation: Both tools are primarily text‑based; database interaction is a feature of Autofill, not Writer.

options.text: Writer generates images, while Q&A generates tables
options.explanation: Neither tool is described as an image generator.

options.text: Writer is a template library, while Q&A is a chatbot
options.explanation: Writer is an AI assistant for drafting, not a template collection.

questions.question: When a product team asks, “What have been the top feature requests from enterprise customers in Q4?” how does Q&A generate the answer?

options.text: It locates the relevant research pages, aggregates the requests, and calculates which ones appear most frequently
options.explanation: The guide states Q&A can synthesize multiple pages of user research to surface insights like top feature requests.

options.text: It creates a fictional list based on general industry trends
options.explanation: Q&A does not fabricate data; it extracts answers from existing workspace content.

options.text: It returns only the titles of the pages that mention “feature requests”
options.explanation: The assistant goes beyond simple page titles to summarize and rank the information.

options.text: It asks a human teammate to provide the answer and forwards it back
options.explanation: Q&A works autonomously using the workspace knowledge base, not by delegating to a person.

questions.question: How does Autofill differ from Q&A in terms of the type of data it handles?

options.text: Autofill extracts and summarizes key information directly from database entries, updating automatically as the database changes
options.explanation: Autofill works at the database property level, providing live summaries and insights.

options.text: Autofill retrieves whole pages of text and rewrites them in a shorter form
options.explanation: That description matches Q&A’s summarization ability, not Autofill.

options.text: Autofill pulls data from external APIs while Q&A only uses internal pages
options.explanation: Autofill does not call external APIs; it works on data already inside Notion databases.

options.text: Autofill generates new content based on prompts, whereas Q&A only answers questions
options.explanation: Content generation is the role of Writer; Autofill focuses on extracting existing data.

questions.question: Which of the following steps is recommended for building a company knowledge hub that supports Q&A?

options.text: Create a company home/wiki, build team‑specific wikis, use a connected project‑and‑task template, and import content from other tools
options.explanation: All listed actions are mentioned as best practices for a robust knowledge hub.

options.text: Only import content from other tools; do not create any internal wikis
options.explanation: The guide advises both importing and creating internal wikis.

options.text: Rely solely on the Q&A assistant without organizing any documentation
options.explanation: Q&A depends on well‑structured documentation; it cannot replace a knowledge base.

options.text: Disable all templates and build everything from scratch
options.explanation: The guide promotes using Notion’s templates to accelerate knowledge‑hub creation.
